markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Assign stable IDs to blocks and embeds for CRDT references, citations, backlinks, and visual-debug selectors. This MT belongs to RichDocumentCore. Build the canonical structured document model backing the editor, Loom, UserManual, and projections.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Rich documents are PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT-backed structured block documents, not Markdown vault authority.
All rich document state must preserve Raw/Derived/Display separation and stable entity IDs.
scope.expected_behavior: BlockStableIdStrategy leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: BlockStableIdStrategy is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified (commits b506d832 backend core + 935e0482 manual + bf0c62a5 integration tests + dd3056f5 frontend); RichDocumentCore: 6 real-PG integration + 27 unit backend tests + 8 vitest (app suite 349/349). 18 block kinds, RawDerivedDisplay (CX-100), stable KBL- ids, save/load API w/ receipts, projections (negative proof: projection-delete leaves authority byte-identical), embeds (absolute paths DB-CHECK rejected), backlinks (stable KDLNK- relationship_id), history, permissions, crash recovery; migrations 0280-0282; RichDocumentView.tsx wired to real authority with stable selectors for MT-161+ GUI groups. UserManual coverage added (1 group + 10 cmd refs).

findings.title: Derived block id is position+text seeded, so it churns on structural edits
findings.evidence: block_tree.rs:318-325 derive_block_id hashes (doc_id, position_path content[i], plain_text). Inserting/reordering a block changes content[i] for every block below -> new ids. Backend never writes derived ids back (to_document_json emits raw verbatim), so stability depends on the FRONTEND persisting attrs.block_id, which is unverified here.
findings.fix: On save, backend should inject derived block_id into each block attrs.block_id when absent so a re-load preserves it; otherwise CRDT refs/citations/backlinks (MT-155 relationship_id embeds block_id) re-key on every structural edit.

claim_mismatches.claim: stable KBL- ids used by CRDT refs, citations, backlinks across loads
claim_mismatches.reality: stable across re-load of identical content only; not stable across edits that shift block position unless the editor round-trips attrs.block_id (no backend test proves the editor does)

missing_tests: test: insert a block at the top, re-parse, assert untouched blocks keep their ids (currently they would not, since position_path shifts)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-148 (BlockStableIdStrategy) requires deterministic, stable IDs for rich-document content blocks/embeds used by CRDT refs, citations, and backlinks. The code delivers a real, deterministic KBL- id strategy (injective length-prefixed sha256 preimage) with a preserve-existing-else-derive policy, wired into backlink relationship_ids and embed block_id, and proven against real managed PostgreSQL. A known design limitation (derived ids re-key on structural edits because the frontend does not round-trip attrs.block_id) is a MEDIUM quality concern, not a placeholder/spec-contradiction/mock-only failure.
findings: block_tree.rs:336-343 derive_block_id: real deterministic id = 'KBL-' + sha256_hex over a length-prefixed injective preimage of (rich_document_id, position_path, plain_text). Same discipline as derive_knowledge_relationship_id. Not a stub.

block_tree.rs:388-395 parse_block: correct preserve-else-derive policy — an existing non-empty attrs.block_id is preserved; otherwise the deterministic id is derived. This is the CRDT-friendly strategy the MT asks for.

backlink.rs:164-189 derive_document_link_relationship_id incorporates block_id, so backlink relationship_ids are stable iff block_id is stable — the strategy is actually consumed downstream (MT-155), not isolated.

knowledge_document_model_tests.rs:108-121 (real managed PostgreSQL via pg.db): asserts block_ids are KBL- prefixed and deterministic across re-parse; :234-242 asserts a backlink relationship_id derived from a block_id is persisted and reverse-lookupable. Real Handshake-owned PG/EventLedger touch — satisfies sub_rule_2; not mock-only.
sub_rule_1: grep of block_tree.rs/backlink.rs/embed.rs found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable escapes on the live path.
sub_rule_3: lifecycle.ready_by=KERNEL_BUILDER, completed_by=null, status=READY_FOR_VALIDATION — not self-certified to COMPLETED.

CONCERN (MEDIUM, non-blocking): The self-review (non-authoritative) is correct that derived ids churn on structural edits. save_document (knowledge_documents.rs:984-994) persists body.content_json verbatim and never injects derived block_id back into attrs.block_id; the frontend Tiptap extension set (app/src/lib/tiptap/) defines no global attrs.block_id, so attrs.block_id is effectively always absent and ids re-derive from position_path on every load. Inserting/reordering a top-level block shifts position_path for all following blocks, re-keying their ids (and their backlink relationship_ids). The integration test only edits text inside an existing block (tests:162-163), so the insert/reorder stability case is unproven. This is a design tradeoff/quality gap, not a placeholder or spec violation — the spec's content-block model (02-system-architecture §2.2.1/§2.2.1.14) does not mandate position-independent intra-document content-block identity, and MT-148 acceptance criteria are 'implemented or specified with enough detail', which the code meets.
Aside (non-blocking, expected): packet cites v02.192 anchors; resolved against current v02.194 — section numbers align; pure paperwork drift, not a code defect.

FOLLOW-UP (MEDIUM, INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR-resolved split): two independent reviewers split on this MT. Reviewer-1 read the position+text-seeded block id (block_tree.rs derive_block_id over doc_id+position_path+plain_text) as FAIL/spec-DRIFT because ids re-key on insert/reorder and the frontend never round-trips attrs.block_id, so backlink relationship_ids (backlink.rs:164) and visual-debug data-stable-id selectors churn. Reviewer-2 (fresh full-context re-review) read it as PASS: the strategy is real, deterministic, PG-proven, no placeholder/mock/escape, and the MT acceptance criteria are the generic implemented-or-specified form, which is met; the churn is a documented MEDIUM design tradeoff (already in this contract lifecycle.adversarial_review_v2). IV RESOLUTION = PASS_V2 with this recorded follow-up. RECOMMENDED FIX (carry into a future MT when reorder-stability is exercised by backlinks/CRDT): Fix the block-id strategy so ids are stable across edits and structural moves, per Master Spec 02-system-architecture.md:323/337 and LM-LINK-003. Concrete steps: (1) Stop seeding the id from text/position. In app/src/lib/tiptap/extension_set.ts add a per-block global-attribute extension (e.g. Tiptap UniqueID or an addGlobalAttributes block that injects attrs.block_id = a UUID — prefer UUID v7 per project direction — at node creation if absent) so every top-level block carries a persistent attrs.block_id BEFORE save. (2) On save, guarantee persistence of that id: either rely on the editor-supplied attrs.block_id (now always present) OR have api/knowledge_documents.rs save_document() inject a fresh stable id into each block's attrs.block_id when absent (mutating the content_json that is persisted via save_knowledge_rich_document_version, knowledge_documents.rs:984-994), so a re-load reproduces the same id regardless of later edits to other blocks. (3) In block_tree.rs derive_block_id(), drop position_path and plain_text from the seed for the fallback case, or remove the fallback entirely once the editor always supplies ids; an id that depends on sibling positions or block text cannot be stable. (4) Required runtime proof: add a real-PG integration test in src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_document_model_tests.rs that creates a doc, records block A's id, saves; then inserts a new block at the TOP and edits an unrelated block's text, saves again, reloads, and asserts block A's block_id is byte-identical to the original AND its backlink relationship_ids (backlink.rs:164) are unchanged. This must pass against the managed PostgreSQL cluster. Until untouched blocks provably keep their ids across an insert/reorder/edit and that survives a PG round-trip, MT-148's 'stable block id' claim is not met.
evidence_checked: app/src/lib/tiptap/ extension set (no attrs.block_id global attribute)

followup_concern.title: Block id churns on structural edit/reorder; backlinks + visual-debug selectors re-key
followup_concern.recommended_fix: Fix the block-id strategy so ids are stable across edits and structural moves, per Master Spec 02-system-architecture.md:323/337 and LM-LINK-003. Concrete steps: (1) Stop seeding the id from text/position. In app/src/lib/tiptap/extension_set.ts add a per-block global-attribute extension (e.g. Tiptap UniqueID or an addGlobalAttributes block that injects attrs.block_id = a UUID — prefer UUID v7 per project direction — at node creation if absent) so every top-level block carries a persistent attrs.block_id BEFORE save. (2) On save, guarantee persistence of that id: either rely on the editor-supplied attrs.block_id (now always present) OR have api/knowledge_documents.rs save_document() inject a fresh stable id into each block's attrs.block_id when absent (mutating the content_json that is persisted via save_knowledge_rich_document_version, knowledge_documents.rs:984-994), so a re-load reproduces the same id regardless of later edits to other blocks. (3) In block_tree.rs derive_block_id(), drop position_path and plain_text from the seed for the fallback case, or remove the fallback entirely once the editor always supplies ids; an id that depends on sibling positions or block text cannot be stable. (4) Required runtime proof: add a real-PG integration test in src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_document_model_tests.rs that creates a doc, records block A's id, saves; then inserts a new block at the TOP and edits an unrelated block's text, saves again, reloads, and asserts block A's block_id is byte-identical to the original AND its backlink relationship_ids (backlink.rs:164) are unchanged. This must pass against the managed PostgreSQL cluster. Until untouched blocks provably keep their ids across an insert/reorder/edit and that survives a PG round-trip, MT-148's 'stable block id' claim is not met.
followup_concern.resolved_by: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR split-resolution: PASS_V2 with documented follow-up

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
